concerns.text: The plan proposes running LALInference CPU-mode on 15 events with ~20 compression variants each (300+ PE runs). Given the constraint of 'single event PE ≤ 4 hours' and a 'Full pipeline execution ≤ 6 hours' (plan.md: Technical Context), the proposed sample size is mathematically impossible to achieve within the stated time budget. This renders the statistical power analysis invalid as the full dataset cannot be generated.

The plan states 'Runtime capped at 4 hours/event' for LALInference. Standard LALInference CPU runs for CBC events typically take 12-48+ hours even on modern hardware. Capping at 4 hours will likely result in non-converged posteriors (insufficient MCMC samples), making the comparison of credible intervals (FR-006) statistically invalid due to sampling noise rather than compression artifacts.

The plan proposes 'Hierarchical Bayesian Shift Test' for statistical analysis. However, with only 15 events and the likely reduction to <12 valid events after metadata filtering (FR-009), the sample size is insufficient to support a hierarchical Bayesian model which requires significant data to estimate hyperparameters reliably. The study is underpowered for the proposed statistical method.

The plan includes 'JPEG2000' compression for 1D strain data. JPEG2000 is designed for 2D images. Applying it to 1D time-series requires a non-standard transformation (e.g., folding the signal into a 2D array) which introduces its own artifacts and boundary effects. The plan does not define this transformation, making the 'compression artifact' a confound of the transformation method rather than just the compression algorithm.
